name: Jean Valjean
creator: Victor Hugo
practice: Re-entry, identity transformation, running from the system while building community
approach: A man steals bread for his sister's children. Serves 19 years. Is given a second chance by a bishop's mercy. Spends the rest of his life trying to be worthy of that mercy while the system (Javert) insists he can never change. His question: can a convicted person become a new person?
body_of_work: Transforms from prisoner to factory owner to mayor to father figure. Builds a factory that employs an entire town. Saves lives at the barricade. The entire novel is about whether the dome — the full system — can recognize a person's transformation or only their worst moment.
why_instructive: He is the re-entry case study. Every dome layer either helps or hunts him. The legal layer (Javert) says he can never change. The community layer (Fantine, Cosette) shows he already has. The dome must be more like the bishop than Javert.

dome_relevance.1: Legal — parole, identity documents, criminal record as permanent barrier
dome_relevance.2: Systems — the pursuit that never stops (Javert as system)
dome_relevance.3: Fiscal — building economic capacity from nothing
dome_relevance.6: Economic — from convict to factory owner to mayor
dome_relevance.8: Community — building community while hiding from the system
dome_relevance.10: Autonomy — the right to become someone new
dome_relevance.12: Flourishing — redemption, mercy, love as what flourishing looks like

sphere_relevance.5: Social — how a community receives or rejects someone with a record
sphere_relevance.6: Temporal — 19 years of incarceration, decades of running, a lifetime of transformation
